project: kcl/tarray — port of FPC rtl-generics TArrayHelper (Sort/BinarySearch/scan helpers) to bash (kklass static class over caller-named arrays)
status: PLANNED — plan + ledger only, no implementation yet. Roadmap position 3/7.
notes: Single source of truth for tarray status. Plan: kcl/tarray/PLAN.md. Source: generics.collections.pas TCustomArrayHelper :73-102 (+TBinarySearchResult :68-71), TArrayHelper :106-138; impl introsort ~:990-1220, BinarySearch :1225-1310/:1457-1520. Positioning: NOT a speed contest with /usr/bin/sort — the wins are zero forks + lossless arbitrary-string elements (newline-safe, where line-based sort corrupts) + binary search; niche n <= ~5k. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap units 1-2 will have raised the totals.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: tarray work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new tarray suite adds on top.

design_decisions.by_name_dense: Static methods over caller INDEXED array names via declare -n (__ta_ local prefix; __ta_* caller names rejected). Density contract 0..n-1 (FPC data model); sparse input NOT silently compacted — explicit TArray.compact; density check only under VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug. Assoc arrays rejected via ${arr@a} (rc=1).
design_decisions.default_comparators: String mode = BYTE order under scoped LC_ALL=C (deterministic across machines; locale collation is a documented non-goal). Numeric mode (-n) = 64-bit integer via (( )); non-integer element -> rc=1, NO silent lexicographic fallback. 10# guards against octal.
design_decisions.custom_comparator: Plain bash function (NOT a kklass method — 0.5 ms dispatch x n log n would be catastrophic). Protocol: cmpFn a b -> return code 0 (a<b) / 1 (equal) / 2 (a>b); no stdout (echo would force a $() fork per comparison). Built-in modes are INLINED in the merge loop.
design_decisions.algorithm: Bottom-up iterative MERGESORT: no recursion, O(n log n) worst case (matches introsort's goal), STABLE. Parity: FPC introsort's equal-element order is UNSPECIFIED, so stability is a strictly stronger documented guarantee (bash-side improvement row). O(n) scratch is fine at target sizes.
design_decisions.binary_search_shape: One call yields both FPC overload shapes: rc 0/1 + RESULT=FoundIndex(-1 miss) + RESULT_CANDIDATE + RESULT_COMPARE (TBinarySearchResult :68-71 flattened to RESULT_* convention). Empty array -> (-1,-1,0,rc1) verbatim :1231-1237. Sorted-under-same-comparator precondition (GIGO like FPC, documented).

goals: Faithful TArrayHelper surface: sort (3 forms), binarySearch (both result shapes), indexOf/firstIndexOf/lastIndexOf, contains, min/max (aDefault), copy (2 forms), reverse (copy-into-target), concat; extras reverseInPlace/compact documented as bash-convention.
Zero forks everywhere incl. the comparator hot path (rc-protocol); PATH='' tests.
Lossless arbitrary-string elements: '', newlines, globs, unicode — the correctness edge over line-based sort.
Determinism: byte-order default, no locale drift, stable sort.

test_basis.seed_primary: rtl-generics/tests checked for array-helper fpcunit coverage at P0 (mined if present). Primary basis: implementation-source reading (line refs in PLAN) + linear-scan oracles.

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Sorted output, search indices/candidates, scan indices, min/max picks, copy/reverse/concat contents — byte-for-byte vs source-pinned semantics and oracles.

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Non-FPC cases (torture elements, stability proof, sparse/assoc rejects, zero-fork) -> TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md rows.

out_of_scope.reason: Comparator is a plain bash function with an rc protocol; object protocol is dispatch overhead with no gain.
The algorithm is not the contract; the sorted result is. Bottom-up mergesort delivers the same O(n log n) bound plus stability.
Elements are bash strings; -n covers int64; float ordering routes through kcl/math in user comparators (README example), not here.

execution_log.summary: tarray PLAN.md + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Key decisions recorded: bottom-up stable mergesort (vs FPC introsort — equal-order unspecified there, so stability is parity-compatible and stronger), comparator = plain function with rc 0/1/2 protocol (fork-free, dispatch-free), byte-order default under scoped LC_ALL=C, dense-array contract with explicit compact, TBinarySearchResult flattened to RESULT_* in one call.
